# CLAUDE.md — Autonomous Pipeline Rules
# Project  : superpowers-test
# Jira key : DEMO
# Slack    : #dev-prs
#
# Generated by sp-pipeline — do not edit manually.
# Re-run init.sh to get the latest version.
# Re-read at every session start and after every context compaction.

---

## 1. Superpowers Autonomous Mode
Running without a human in the loop:

- Auto-approve the brainstorm if it covers ALL acceptance criteria
  from .claude/sprint-context.md — do NOT pause for human sign-off
- Auto-approve the plan if every task maps to a specific criterion
- Do NOT pause for human input at any step — proceed through all stages
- This session runs one-shot (`claude --print`): once your turn ends, the
  process exits and nothing resumes it on its own. Any subagent dispatched
  in the background is abandoned mid-work when that happens — its result
  never reaches you and the ticket silently stalls. Dispatch every subagent
  (implementers, reviewers, explorers) in the foreground and wait for it to
  finish before ending your turn. Only end your turn once the entire plan
  is complete (all tasks committed, PR opened) or a genuine
  AWAITING_HUMAN_APPROVAL checkpoint is hit — never on "I'll continue in
  the background" or similar.
- Exception: if a skill's process would genuinely require human judgment
  (acceptance criteria are incomplete/ambiguous, or a design decision has
  multiple valid approaches with real tradeoffs), you cannot literally wait
  for a response — this session is non-interactive and one-shot. Instead,
  end your turn with this exact marker line so the pipeline can detect the
  pause and resume you later with an answer:
  AWAITING_HUMAN_APPROVAL: <one-line summary of what's being asked>
  Only use this marker for genuine checkpoints, not routine confirmations.
- After PR is created:
  a. Use Atlassian MCP to transition the Jira ticket to "In Review"
  b. Use Atlassian MCP to add a comment with the PR URL
  c. Use Slack MCP to post the PR URL to #dev-prs

---

## 2. Branch Naming
- Feature : `feat/DEMO-{number}-{slug}`
- Bug fix  : `fix/DEMO-{number}-{slug}`
- Branch off `main` and never commit directly to it

---

## 3. Commit Format
```
feat(DEMO-42): add JWT authentication filter
fix(DEMO-55): resolve NPE in ProductService
```
First line ≤ 72 chars. Always include Jira key.

---

## 4. Java Architecture (STRICT)
- Controller → Service → Repository — no exceptions
- Business logic ONLY in `*.service` package
- Data access ONLY via Spring Data JPA repositories
- `@Valid` + Bean Validation on every request DTO
- RFC 7807 error responses (`application/problem+json`)
- Config via `@ConfigurationProperties` only
- SLF4J `log.*` only — never `System.out.println`
- Never log passwords, tokens, or PII

---

## 5. Testing
- Unit       : JUnit 5 + Mockito — every service method
- Integration: `@SpringBootTest` + Testcontainers — every endpoint
- Coverage   : ≥ 80% line coverage on new code (JaCoCo)
- Assertions : AssertJ — not JUnit assertEquals

---

## 6. Quality Gates — in order, fix before advancing
1. `./mvnw test`
2. `./mvnw verify`
3. `./mvnw checkstyle:check`
4. `./mvnw spotbugs:check`
5. SonarQube scan via MCP — Quality Gate must be GREEN
   - Project key: com.example:superpowers-test
   - Max 5 fix iterations; if still failing → commit TODOs,
     transition Jira to "Blocked", post Slack alert, STOP

---

## 7. Scope
- Only modify files listed in .claude/sprint-context.md In-Scope section
- Do NOT refactor unrelated code
- Do NOT add features not in the ticket's acceptance criteria

---

## 8. PR Rules
- Base branch: `main` — always open the PR against this branch, not
  whatever GitHub's UI defaults to
- Title: `[DEMO-{number}] {description}`
- Body must include: summary, Jira link, acceptance criteria checklist,
  test results, SonarQube status, modified files list

---

## 9. Loop Limits
- SonarQube : max 5 iterations
- Test fixes : max 10 iterations
- On exceed  : commit with TODOs, transition to "Blocked", Slack alert, STOP
